title: Intermediate Applied Mathematics: Models and Data

audience.description: Learners with arithmetic and introductory algebra fluency who want rigorous function, data, probability, optimization, network, and computational modeling skill.
entryKnowledge: Complete Beginner Applied Mathematics or demonstrate fractions, percent, equations, graphs, units, and introductory Python skill.
deviceConstraints: Desktop or tablet browser with the built-in Python workspace; a local scientific Python environment is optional.
accessibilityAssumptions: All equations, diagrams, tables, terminal traces, and code examples require equivalent structured text and full keyboard operation.

placementEvidence: A placement review or prior artifact must demonstrate this entry skill: Complete Beginner Applied Mathematics or demonstrate fractions, percent, equations, graphs, units, and introductory Python skill.
completionEvidence: Complete and defend all 4 stakeholder projects against their acceptance constraints and rubrics.
Pass the cumulative performance examination at or above 85 percent with changed-case evidence.

includes: Algebra and trigonometry through model comparison
Probability, inference, optimization, matrices, and graph methods

sources.title: OpenStax Algebra and Trigonometry 2e
sources.scope: Controls prerequisite order across algebra, functions, systems, trigonometry, sequences, and probability.

sources.title: OpenStax Introductory Statistics 2e
sources.scope: Controls introductory inference, regression, and application scope.

sources.title: NumPy Documentation
sources.scope: Controls arrays, numerical operations, linear algebra, random simulation, and testing APIs.

sources.title: ASA GAISE Reports
sources.version: GAISE II and College revision materials
sources.scope: Controls simulation-first statistical reasoning and interpretation.

competencies.statement: Turn stakeholder needs into answerable model questions, quantities, constraints, and validation criteria.
misconceptions: A stakeholder request arrives as a complete mathematical problem.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Turn stakeholder needs into answerable model questions, quantities, constraints, and validation criteria.

competencies.statement: Track units, scales, significant digits, and uncertainty through multi-stage calculations.
misconceptions: Units can be restored after a dimensionless calculation.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Track units, scales, significant digits, and uncertainty through multi-stage calculations.

competencies.statement: Estimate how measurement and rounding error affect a computed result.
misconceptions: Small input errors never matter in a long calculation.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Estimate how measurement and rounding error affect a computed result.

competencies.statement: Classify structural, parameter, and data assumptions and test material ones.
misconceptions: All assumptions have equal influence on a conclusion.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Classify structural, parameter, and data assumptions and test material ones.

competencies.statement: Validate a model against held-out cases, limits, units, and stakeholder behavior.
misconceptions: A model is valid when its training examples fit.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Validate a model against held-out cases, limits, units, and stakeholder behavior.

competencies.statement: Transform expressions and equations while stating domain restrictions and preserving equivalence.
misconceptions: Any canceled factor may be ignored for every input.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Transform expressions and equations while stating domain restrictions and preserving equivalence.

competencies.statement: Solve rational and radical equations and reject introduced or excluded solutions.
misconceptions: Every algebraically produced solution satisfies the original equation.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Solve rational and radical equations and reject introduced or excluded solutions.

competencies.statement: Model thresholds and distance with absolute-value and piecewise relationships.
misconceptions: A piecewise function is discontinuous at every boundary.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Model thresholds and distance with absolute-value and piecewise relationships.

competencies.statement: Solve and graph compound and two-variable inequality systems as feasible regions.
misconceptions: Testing one point proves an entire boundary is feasible.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Solve and graph compound and two-variable inequality systems as feasible regions.

competencies.statement: Analyze how parameter changes move roots, intersections, and feasible ranges.
misconceptions: Parameters are fixed constants with no modeling interpretation.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Analyze how parameter changes move roots, intersections, and feasible ranges.

competencies.statement: Define function domain, codomain, range, units, and practical interpretation.
misconceptions: A formula alone fully defines a practical function.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define function domain, codomain, range, units, and practical interpretation.

competencies.statement: Predict how translations, scaling, reflection, and composition change a model.
misconceptions: Graph transformations change every coefficient in the same way.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Predict how translations, scaling, reflection, and composition change a model.

competencies.statement: Construct and validate inverses under one-to-one domain restrictions.
misconceptions: Every function has an inverse function on its original domain.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Construct and validate inverses under one-to-one domain restrictions.

competencies.statement: Compose functions while checking intermediate domain and unit compatibility.
misconceptions: Compatible-looking formulas can always be composed.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compose functions while checking intermediate domain and unit compatibility.

competencies.statement: Reconcile tables, equations, graphs, and code implementations of one function.
misconceptions: Visual agreement at a few points proves implementations equivalent.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Reconcile tables, equations, graphs, and code implementations of one function.

competencies.statement: Fit and interpret linear rate and intercept parameters with units and domain limits.
misconceptions: A nonzero intercept is always meaningful.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Fit and interpret linear rate and intercept parameters with units and domain limits.

competencies.statement: Fit a linear regression and inspect residuals before using its predictions.
misconceptions: A high correlation guarantees a suitable linear model.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Fit a linear regression and inspect residuals before using its predictions.

competencies.statement: Solve linear systems and classify unique, absent, and infinite solutions.
misconceptions: Every pair of linear equations intersects once.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Solve linear systems and classify unique, absent, and infinite solutions.

competencies.statement: Represent and solve systems with augmented matrices and row operations.
misconceptions: Row operations change the solution set.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Represent and solve systems with augmented matrices and row operations.

competencies.statement: Use sensitivity and shadow interpretations to defend a constrained linear decision.

masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use sensitivity and shadow interpretations to defend a constrained linear decision.

competencies.statement: Relate polynomial degree, roots, multiplicity, end behavior, and turning points.
misconceptions: A polynomial graph can cross each root only once.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Relate polynomial degree, roots, multiplicity, end behavior, and turning points.

competencies.statement: Fit and critique polynomial models without extrapolating unsupported behavior.
misconceptions: Higher degree always produces a better predictive model.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Fit and critique polynomial models without extrapolating unsupported behavior.

competencies.statement: Interpret zeros, holes, asymptotes, and domain restrictions in rational models.
misconceptions: An asymptote is a line a graph can never cross.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Interpret zeros, holes, asymptotes, and domain restrictions in rational models.

competencies.statement: Find nonlinear roots graphically and numerically and verify them in the original model.
misconceptions: A calculator root is exact and unique.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Find nonlinear roots graphically and numerically and verify them in the original model.

competencies.statement: Compare nonlinear candidates using residual patterns, simplicity, mechanism, and validation.
misconceptions: The curve with the smallest training error must be selected.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare nonlinear candidates using residual patterns, simplicity, mechanism, and validation.

competencies.statement: Distinguish additive change from multiplicative growth and derive period factors.
misconceptions: A constant percentage produces a linear graph.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish additive change from multiplicative growth and derive period factors.

competencies.statement: Fit exponential growth and decay parameters with explicit initial conditions.
misconceptions: Every decreasing process follows exponential decay.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Fit exponential growth and decay parameters with explicit initial conditions.

competencies.statement: Use logarithms as inverses to solve for time, rate, or exponent.
misconceptions: Logarithms are merely calculator buttons without model meaning.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use logarithms as inverses to solve for time, rate, or exponent.

competencies.statement: Interpret orders of magnitude and changes on logarithmic measurement scales.
misconceptions: Equal distances on a log scale represent equal additive changes.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Interpret orders of magnitude and changes on logarithmic measurement scales.

competencies.statement: Identify saturation, regime changes, and extrapolation risks in exponential models.
misconceptions: Exponential growth can continue indefinitely in a finite system.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Identify saturation, regime changes, and extrapolation risks in exponential models.

competencies.statement: Use radians and the unit circle to define trigonometric functions and periodicity.
misconceptions: Degrees are intrinsic inputs to every trigonometric formula.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use radians and the unit circle to define trigonometric functions and periodicity.

competencies.statement: Apply right-triangle ratios with diagrams, units, and angle conditions.
misconceptions: Any known angle and side always determine a unique triangle.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Apply right-triangle ratios with diagrams, units, and angle conditions.

competencies.statement: Choose laws of sines or cosines and detect ambiguous cases.
misconceptions: The law of sines always returns one physical configuration.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose laws of sines or cosines and detect ambiguous cases.

competencies.statement: Resolve vectors into components and reconstruct magnitude and direction.
misconceptions: Vector magnitudes can be added regardless of direction.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Resolve vectors into components and reconstruct magnitude and direction.

competencies.statement: Fit amplitude, period, phase, and midline to a periodic dataset.
misconceptions: Every repeating pattern is exactly sinusoidal.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Fit amplitude, period, phase, and midline to a periodic dataset.

competencies.statement: Represent explicit and recursive sequences and state valid index domains.
misconceptions: An explicit formula and recurrence require identical inputs.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Represent explicit and recursive sequences and state valid index domains.

competencies.statement: Model constant differences and constant ratios with arithmetic and geometric sequences.
misconceptions: Any increasing sequence is geometric.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Model constant differences and constant ratios with arithmetic and geometric sequences.

competencies.statement: Calculate finite sums and explain their accumulated quantity and units.
misconceptions: A sequence term and its partial sum are the same quantity.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Calculate finite sums and explain their accumulated quantity and units.

competencies.statement: Build and simulate recurrence relations with initial conditions and constraints.
misconceptions: A recurrence can run without an initial state.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Build and simulate recurrence relations with initial conditions and constraints.

competencies.statement: Inspect long-run behavior and sensitivity of discrete models.
misconceptions: A small one-step error remains small forever.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Inspect long-run behavior and sensitivity of discrete models.

competencies.statement: Choose multiplication, permutations, combinations, or inclusion-exclusion from event structure.
misconceptions: All counting problems can be solved by factorials alone.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose multiplication, permutations, combinations, or inclusion-exclusion from event structure.

competencies.statement: Update probabilities with conditional information and Bayes reasoning.
misconceptions: A positive result probability equals the condition probability in reverse.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Update probabilities with conditional information and Bayes reasoning.

competencies.statement: Test independence and distinguish it from mutually exclusive events.
misconceptions: Events that cannot co-occur are independent.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Test independence and distinguish it from mutually exclusive events.

competencies.statement: Define discrete random variables and calculate expectation and variance.
misconceptions: Expected value must be a possible observed value.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define discrete random variables and calculate expectation and variance.

competencies.statement: Design reproducible simulations and quantify Monte Carlo sampling error.
misconceptions: One large simulation produces an exact probability.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Design reproducible simulations and quantify Monte Carlo sampling error.

competencies.statement: Distinguish observational studies, experiments, sampling frames, randomization, and blocking.
misconceptions: Random sampling and random assignment serve the same purpose.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish observational studies, experiments, sampling frames, randomization, and blocking.

competencies.statement: Explain sampling variability through simulated sampling distributions.
misconceptions: A sample statistic has no uncertainty once calculated.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Explain sampling variability through simulated sampling distributions.

competencies.statement: Construct and interpret confidence intervals without probability misstatements.
misconceptions: A computed interval has the stated probability of containing a fixed parameter.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Construct and interpret confidence intervals without probability misstatements.

competencies.statement: Define hypotheses, errors, significance, effect size, and decision limits.
misconceptions: A small p-value measures practical importance.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define hypotheses, errors, significance, effect size, and decision limits.

competencies.statement: Inspect residuals, leverage, confounding, and extrapolation before regression claims.
misconceptions: Regression software validates causal conclusions automatically.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Inspect residuals, leverage, confounding, and extrapolation before regression claims.

competencies.statement: Use matrix dimensions to validate addition, multiplication, and transformations.
misconceptions: Matrix multiplication is element-by-element multiplication.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use matrix dimensions to validate addition, multiplication, and transformations.

competencies.statement: Interpret matrices as transformations of vectors and coordinate systems.
misconceptions: A matrix is only a rectangular storage table.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Interpret matrices as transformations of vectors and coordinate systems.

competencies.statement: Solve invertible systems and distinguish existence from numerical stability.
misconceptions: A nonzero determinant guarantees a reliable numerical solution.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Solve invertible systems and distinguish existence from numerical stability.

competencies.statement: Interpret determinants as signed scale factors and singularity evidence.
misconceptions: The determinant is merely a memorized arithmetic recipe.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Interpret determinants as signed scale factors and singularity evidence.

competencies.statement: Build transition, network, and resource matrices from domain relationships.
misconceptions: Matrix orientation does not affect the meaning of a model.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Build transition, network, and resource matrices from domain relationships.

competencies.statement: Define decision variables, objective, constraints, units, and feasible domain.
misconceptions: Optimization begins by selecting an algorithm.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define decision variables, objective, constraints, units, and feasible domain.

competencies.statement: Solve two-variable linear programs graphically and verify corner candidates.
misconceptions: The optimum must occur where both axes are positive.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Solve two-variable linear programs graphically and verify corner candidates.

competencies.statement: Encode a constrained model for a solver and independently validate its solution.
misconceptions: A solver status of success proves the model matches reality.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Encode a constrained model for a solver and independently validate its solution.

competencies.statement: Analyze active constraints, slack, parameter changes, and competing objectives.
misconceptions: One optimal solution remains best under any parameter change.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Analyze active constraints, slack, parameter changes, and competing objectives.

competencies.statement: Evaluate whose costs and benefits an objective includes or omits.
misconceptions: A mathematically optimal plan is automatically fair.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Evaluate whose costs and benefits an objective includes or omits.

competencies.statement: Explain floating-point approximation and compare values with justified tolerances.
misconceptions: Binary floating-point stores every decimal exactly.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Explain floating-point approximation and compare values with justified tolerances.

competencies.statement: Use NumPy arrays, shapes, dtypes, indexing, and vectorized operations intentionally.
misconceptions: A NumPy array behaves exactly like a nested Python list.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use NumPy arrays, shapes, dtypes, indexing, and vectorized operations intentionally.

competencies.statement: Choose visual encodings, scales, labels, and uncertainty displays that support the question.
misconceptions: A decorative chart is evidence even when scales and units are absent.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose visual encodings, scales, labels, and uncertainty displays that support the question.

competencies.statement: Test numerical code with invariants, reference cases, tolerances, and randomized cases.
misconceptions: Exact equality is the correct assertion for all computed floats.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Test numerical code with invariants, reference cases, tolerances, and randomized cases.

competencies.statement: Pin inputs and environments and separate exploration from rerunnable analysis.
misconceptions: A notebook that ran once is inherently reproducible.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Pin inputs and environments and separate exploration from rerunnable analysis.

competencies.statement: Represent vertices, edges, direction, weights, and attributes from a real system.
misconceptions: Every network edge should be undirected and equally weighted.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Represent vertices, edges, direction, weights, and attributes from a real system.

competencies.statement: Analyze paths, cycles, components, reachability, and bottlenecks.
misconceptions: A connected network guarantees short routes.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Analyze paths, cycles, components, reachability, and bottlenecks.

competencies.statement: Use trees and spanning structures for hierarchy and low-cost connectivity.
misconceptions: Every connected subgraph is a tree.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use trees and spanning structures for hierarchy and low-cost connectivity.

competencies.statement: Compute shortest paths and verify that edge assumptions fit the algorithm.
misconceptions: The route with the fewest edges always has minimum cost.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compute shortest paths and verify that edge assumptions fit the algorithm.

competencies.statement: Compare efficiency, resilience, fairness, and capacity in network decisions.
misconceptions: A globally shortest route cannot overload shared resources.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare efficiency, resilience, fairness, and capacity in network decisions.

competencies.statement: Decompose an unfamiliar decision into linked algebraic, statistical, and computational submodels.
misconceptions: One familiar formula should control the whole problem.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Decompose an unfamiliar decision into linked algebraic, statistical, and computational submodels.

competencies.statement: Compare multiple plausible models with validation evidence and complexity costs.
misconceptions: Model selection can be based on fit alone.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare multiple plausible models with validation evidence and complexity costs.

competencies.statement: Propagate uncertainty through best, expected, and adverse scenarios.
misconceptions: Scenario analysis is equivalent to predicting three futures.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Propagate uncertainty through best, expected, and adverse scenarios.

competencies.statement: Explain methods and limitations with equations, code, visuals, and plain-language implications.
misconceptions: Technical correctness removes the need for audience adaptation.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Explain methods and limitations with equations, code, visuals, and plain-language implications.

competencies.statement: Document data provenance, revisions, approvals, and conditions requiring model retirement.
misconceptions: A deployed model needs no ownership or maintenance plan.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Document data provenance, revisions, approvals, and conditions requiring model retirement.
